sound_type: sustained high-pitched territorial scream: begins high and gets higher; sustained duration (not a brief call); primary energy 1,500-8,000 Hz; nocturnal onset without warning; most acoustically startling sound per unit body mass of any mammal in the SPS engine; produced by animal under 1.5 kg in old-growth forest at night
ecosystem: Old-growth forest -- large-diameter trees, complex canopy, significant dead wood (snags and logs), dense understory; Pacific Marten is an old-growth structural obligate; requires features produced only by centuries of forest development
season: Nocturnal (year-round; territorial screaming at night in response to intruder or during breeding season disputes; most intense territorial activity varies by season)
conservation_status: Least Concern overall (IUCN), but sensitive in southern range (Sierra Nevada, southern Cascades). Old-growth structural dependent. Old-growth quality indicator species.
milestone: FIRST MUSTELIDAE AT MAXIMUM. E=9 = SPS MAXIMUM. The highest energy entry in the entire SPS engine. MELVINS-MARTEN EQUIVALENCE: both Aberdeen-adjacent, both from deep Pacific Northwest, both at maximum energy on their respective tracks. ENGINE DOUBLE MAXIMUM ZONE: S36 E=8 (Melvins) + SPS E=9 (Marten) = E=17, the engine's highest combined energy degree.

coordinates.description: Pacific Northwest distribution: Cascade Range, Olympic Peninsula, Coast Ranges, Klamath Mountains. Requires old-growth or mature coniferous forest with structural complexity. Territory 2-5 km² for females. Confirmed presence in mature forests throughout the PNW. Absent from young plantation forests. Old-growth indicator.

acoustic_data.vocalization_type: Territorial scream: syrinx-produced sustained high-pitched vocalization. Not a brief call -- a sustained cry that begins high and rises. Produced at high amplitude by a small animal (<1.5 kg). Most extreme acoustic-to-mass ratio in engine's mammal documentation.
acoustic_data.mechanics: High-frequency vocalization (1,500-8,000 Hz primary). Sustained duration: several seconds. High amplitude relative to body mass. Produced in nocturnal context without prior warning sounds (no footsteps, no snapping branches). Sudden onset maximizes startle effect.
acoustic_data.frequency_range: Primary energy 1,500-8,000 Hz -- well above fundamental frequencies of most Pacific Northwest mammal vocalizations. High frequency penetrates forest differently from low-frequency calls; unexpected carrying distance through understory.
acoustic_data.duration: Sustained (several seconds); longer than expected for animal of this mass. May be repeated in sequence during territorial dispute.
acoustic_data.e9_calibration: E=9: SPS maximum. Five factors: (1) extreme acoustic intensity per body mass (1.0 kg animal producing E=9 -- most extreme ratio in engine), (2) sustained high-frequency duration, (3) frequency range maximizing acoustic startlement (1,500-8,000 Hz), (4) nocturnal context with no warning (maximum startle), (5) old-growth ecological significance (structural obligate in PNW's most complex ecosystem). Combined: SPS ceiling.

nodes.title: SPS Maximum -- E=9 -- First in Engine
nodes.description: The Pacific Marten territorial scream is the first E=9 entry in the SPS engine. E=9 is the SPS maximum. The most acoustically startling sound per unit body mass of any mammal in the Pacific Northwest: under 1.5 kg, sustained high-pitched scream at 1,500-8,000 Hz, nocturnal, in old-growth.

nodes.title: Engine Double Maximum Zone -- Degree 308
nodes.description: S36 maximum (Melvins, E=8) + SPS maximum (Marten, E=9) = E=17. The engine's highest combined energy degree. The first time both tracks reach their respective ceilings at the same degree. The degree-308 double maximum zone.

nodes.title: Melvins-Marten Equivalence
nodes.description: Geographic: Aberdeen/Montesano surrounded by old-growth (Marten habitat). The logging economy that built Aberdeen removed the old-growth the Marten requires. The band from the logging town paired with the animal that logging destroys. Character: both uncompromising, maximum intensity, on their own terms, indifferent to commercial recognition. Energy: E=8 (Melvins, S36 max) vs E=9 (Marten, SPS max). The forest exceeds the music by one.

nodes.title: Pacific Marten as Old-Growth Structural Obligate
nodes.description: Territory quality correlated directly with old-growth structural characteristics: large-diameter snags (den sites, including Pileated-excavated cavities), down logs (subnivean travel), complex multi-layered canopy (arboreal travel), diverse understory (prey). Cannot maintain full territorial density in young plantation forests. Old-growth indicator species.

nodes.title: Most Extreme Acoustic-to-Mass Ratio in Engine
nodes.description: A Grizzly Bear (300 kg) produces E=8. A Pacific Marten (1.0 kg) produces E=9. 300x size difference; Marten is one unit higher in energy. The most extreme acoustic-to-mass ratio of any mammal in the SPS engine. E=9 is possible because acoustic intensity is not primarily a function of body size for the Marten's vocal apparatus.

cross_references.relationship: E=8 + E=9 = E=17. ENGINE DOUBLE MAXIMUM. Aberdeen geography. Melvins-Marten equivalence: uncompromising, maximum intensity, Aberdeen-adjacent. The forest exceeds the music by one.

Old-growth namesake species creates Marten den cavities (Pileated excavations). Engine's most significant recurring species supports engine's maximum-energy species. Old-growth infrastructure connection.
Old-growth thread: engine begins in old-growth (degree 4), five-sixths-marks in old-growth (300), maximizes in old-growth (308). Old-growth is the SPS engine's ecological spine.
Previous E=8 entry. Grizzly: 300 kg, E=8. Marten: 1.0 kg, E=9. The Marten scream surpasses the Grizzly charge by one unit. Acoustic-to-mass ratio reversal.
